step_by_step_thinking: From the given information, we see that cavity varnish typically contains sodium monofluorophosphate, which acts primarily as a fluoride ion provider. Fluoride has well-documented benefits in inhibiting demineralization and promoting remineralization of tooth enamel, thus preventing cavities. Furthermore, cavity varnishes often serve dual purposes—they help reduce sensitivity caused by exposed dentinal tubules due to erosion or decay, and may contain materials like those found in cavity varnish to seal off open tubules, further reducing pain and discomfort associated with sensitive teeth.
